format: ALEX/JORDAN dialogue, plain text, no stage directions

must_cover_status.seat_term: Council D7, four-year term beginning first Monday in December 2026 (Dec 7); seat created in post-2020 redistricting; Luedtke first ever D7 incumbent
must_cover_status.every_other_candidate_one_paragraph: Luedtke (incumbent paragraph) + Hidayat (former MoCo cop, three-time prior candidate, up-county-equity message); Republican Maldonado waiting in November
must_cover_status.3_5_substantive_disagreements: (1) up-county equity in capital plan, (2) policing posture, (3) establishment optics + cash, (4) sanctuary-policy posture (Luedtke yes, Hidayat unknown)
must_cover_status.listener_topics_in_this_race: sanctuary (Luedtke yes / Hidayat unknown / framework not contested by either Dem); school funding (yes / unspecified-redirect); Wootton (Council does not vote, both candidates have no published position); property tax (neither marquee anti-tax candidate); MCPS rating system (BoE not Council)
must_cover_status.what_changes_if_luedtke_wins: 5 concrete continuities — Public Safety committee continuity / immigration-framework continuity / budget-yes continuity / silence on Wootton+MCPS-grading / continued Eric-Luedtke-Moore-administration optics

ends_with_required_question: yes — 'what concretely changes if Dawn Luedtke wins her second term' addressed in penultimate segment
